title: Initialize git repository, stage all files and create empty .gitignore
description: Initializes a new git repository in current directory, stages all existing files, and creates an empty .gitignore file.
explanation: This command sequence creates a new git repository (git init), adds all files in the current directory to the staging area (git add .), and creates an empty .gitignore file (touch .gitignore) for future ignore patterns.
author: marcos

examples.command: cd /path/to/project && git init && git add . && touch .gitignore

notes: The .gitignore file is empty after creation; you should add patterns to ignore unnecessary files
If .gitignore already exists, touch command updates its timestamp
Use git status to verify files are staged
warnings: git add . stages all files including potentially sensitive data
